steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for a difference in how the two authors view the benefits of the City of Gastronomy designation. Text 1 says "Simply by selecting Mérida, UNESCO has helped bring awareness... and has provided a significant boost" — benefits flow automatically from selection. Text 2 counters with "However, cities must still create effective marketing strategies to benefit fully" — benefits require intentional effort.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "The author of Text 1 considers the effects of Mérida’s City of Gastronomy status to be less permanent than the author of Text 2 considers them to be.": Neither text makes any claim about how permanent the benefits are. ✗
  • "The author of Text 1 states that the benefits are automatic, while the author of Text 2 states that effort is required to fully achieve the benefits.": Text 1 uses "simply by selecting" to frame benefits as automatic; Text 2 uses "must still create effective marketing strategies" to frame them as requiring deliberate work. ✓
  • "The author of Text 1 views Mérida’s status as a City of Gastronomy as having been beneficial for the city, but the author of Text 2 questions whether there’s been a benefit.": Text 2 does not question whether benefits exist — it argues that full benefits require effort. ✗
  • "The author of Text 1 assumes that a city’s restaurant owners and chefs are most likely to benefit from a city being named a City of Gastronomy, but the author of Text 2 states that the honor most affects tour guides and tour operators.": Tour guides and tour operators appear nowhere in either text. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
